description: Kanda Shashti Kavasam verse-by-verse meaning explainer. Composed by Devaraya Swamigal, 19th century Tamil devotional hymn to Lord Murugan.

topics.title: Kanda Shashti Kavasam Verse 1 — Thuthiporkku Val Vinaipom
topics.context: Opening invocation: 'Thuthiporkku val vinaipom, thunbam pom nenjil pathiyum kavi.' Meaning: strong negative karma dissolves and sorrow departs for those who praise. Set the tone as promise-first, request-later.

topics.title: Kanda Shashti Kavasam Verse 2 — Sulori Ayilvel
topics.context: Introduces the Vel — Murugan's divine spear. 'Sulori ayilvel sudarvel vezhi.' Discuss the symbolism of Vel as consciousness piercing ignorance. Ground in Tamil Shaiva tradition.

topics.title: Kanda Shashti Kavasam Verse 3 — Pazhaninathan
topics.context: Invocation to Murugan as Pazhaninathan — Lord of Palani hill. Reference the story of the mango and the choice that made Murugan the Palani deity. Verifiable Puranic story.
